title: Carrot & Coriander Soup
keywords: nigel slater carrot coriander soup thermomix
intro: Nigel Slater writes about food like it's poetry, and his carrot and coriander soup is comfort in a bowl. The Thermomix cooks and blends it velvety in the same bowl.
steps: Chop onion, garlic and ginger 4 sec / speed 5. Scrape down.
Add oil and ground coriander and sauté 3 min / 120°C / speed 1.
Add carrots and stock. Cook 20 min / 100°C / speed 1.
Blend 1 min / speed 9 until silky. Season and finish with fresh coriander.
tips: A squeeze of orange juice at the end lifts the carrots beautifully.
Add a swirl of yoghurt or coconut milk for richness.

faq.a: Yes — it's naturally dairy-free; finish with coconut milk instead of yoghurt.

faq.q: Is this the original Nigel Slater recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Nigel Slater and carrot & coriander soup; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.

faq.q: Can I make this Carrot & Coriander Soup in a TM6 or TM7?
faq.a: Yes. The method is written with TM6 and TM7 settings, using metric weights, clear times, temperatures and speeds.

description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Nigel Slater-style carrot & coriander soup: metric ingredients, exact TM6 and TM7 times, temperatures and speeds for Australian home cooks.
